records.ety: [F., fr. L. profectus advance, progress, profit, fr. profectum. See Proficient.]

defs: Acquisition beyond expenditure; excess of value received for producing, keeping, or selling, over cost; hence, pecuniary gain in any transaction or occupation; emolument; as, a profit on the sale of goods.
quotes: Let no man anticipate uncertain profits.

defs: Accession of good; valuable results; useful consequences; benefit; avail; gain; as, an office of profit,
quotes: This I speak for your own profit.
If you dare do yourself a profit and a right.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Profited; p. pr. & vb. n. Profiting.]

defs: To be of service to; to be good to; to help on; to benefit; to advantage; to avail; to aid; as, truth profits all men.
quotes: The word preached did not profit them.
It is a great means of profiting yourself, to copy diligently excellent pieces and beautiful designs.

defs: To gain advantage; to make improvement; to improve; to gain; to advance.
quotes: I profit not by thy talk.

defs: To be of use or advantage; to do or bring good.
quotes: Riches profit not in the day of wrath.
